pattern: Sorting the coins and extending the reachable prefix whenever the next coin is no larger than one past the current reach yields the count of makeable consecutive values.
hints: If you can already make every value in [0, r], a new coin of value c extends that range only when c is at most r+1.
Process coins from smallest to largest so the reachable range only ever grows by contiguous chunks.
Start with reach=0 (only the value 0); for each sorted coin c, if c<=reach+1 add c to reach, else stop; the answer is reach+1.

edge_inputs.description: smallest coin is not one so only zero is makeable

stress.description: random coins over the full value range
